meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code Skills Setup to Finish SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS development. Use this intermediate checklist to master Claude Code and Antigravity for rapid, revenue-focused product delivery.
content.answer_block: To stop the cycle of unfinished projects, you must shift from manual coding to AI-orchestrated engineering. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you automate boilerplate, enforce architectural consistency, and force rapid iterations. This setup transforms your terminal into a high-leverage development environment that prioritizes speed-to-market and revenue-generating features over technical debt.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects—brilliant ideas strangled by the friction of implementation. You start with high-octane ambition, but the momentum dies in the desert of endless refactoring and scope creep. You aren't failing because you lack ideas; you are failing because your development pipeline lacks the velocity to outrun your own doubt.

This isn't about writing more code; it's about building a machine that writes it for you. By combining the autonomous reasoning of Claude Code with the structural scaffolding of Antigravity, you create a feedback loop that forces completion. It is time to stop acting like a junior developer and start operating like a technical architect who ships daily.

steps.title: Environment Hardening
steps.description: Configure your terminal workspace to allow Claude Code persistent access to your project context, ensuring it understands your specific tech stack constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project directory and generate a 'system_instructions.md' file that defines our tech stack, coding standards, and monetization-first architecture.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define high-level feature requirements before writing a single line of business logic to prevent scope drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular architecture diagram for a subscription-based SaaS. Focus on decoupling the auth layer from the payment gateway logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review these architectural constraints and generate the folder structure for a Stripe-integrated SaaS.'

steps.title: Automated Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Eliminate the 'blank page' syndrome by having the AI generate the core scaffold of your database models and API routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CRUD schema for user profiles and subscription status using Prisma and TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create the Prisma schema and initial migration files based on the architecture doc.'

steps.title: Revenue-First Feature Iteration
steps.description: Force the AI to prioritize features that directly contribute to MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) rather than aesthetic polish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the critical path to payment processing and generate the controller logic for handling webhooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Build the checkout flow component. Focus on conversion optimization and error handling for failed transactions.'

steps.title: Final Quality Assurance & Deployment
steps.description: Execute a final sweep to ensure code quality meets production standards before pushing to your CI/CD pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks in the database query layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Perform a security audit and generate a summary report of potential issues.'

pro_tips: Always force Claude Code to output 'Why' before 'What' to ensure the AI understands the business context of your code.
Use a dedicated '.cursorrules' or 'system_instructions' file to inject your specific coding style, forcing consistency across all features.
Treat your terminal session as a 'session-based memory'—clear it every time you pivot to a new feature to avoid context contamination.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'explain the bottleneck' instead of 'fix the bug' to uncover underlying architectural flaws.
Always validate AI-generated code by creating a unit test first; if the test passes, the feature is ready to ship.
content.next_steps: Integrate your local repository with a CI/CD pipeline like Vercel or GitHub Actions to automate your deployment cycle immediately after testing.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical arsenal to ship faster than 99% of your competitors. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; build the MVP that pays for itself. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
